title: St. Luke's International Hospital
description: Major international hospital in central Tokyo with full English-speaking staff. General medicine, pediatrics, OB/GYN, and emergency services.

title: Tokyo Midtown Medical Center
description: Comprehensive medical center in Roppongi with English-speaking doctors. Internal medicine, dermatology, and health checkups.

title: TELL Lifeline & Counseling
description: English-language mental health support including counseling, therapy, and crisis intervention for expats in Japan.

title: RetireJapan Financial Advisory
description: Independent financial advice for foreign residents in Japan. Specializes in NISA, iDeCo, retirement planning, and international investments.

title: Plaza Homes
description: Japan's largest English-language real estate agency with over 55 years in the Tokyo market and 7,000+ property listings. Trusted by embassies and global corporations for expat relocations. Offers apartment rentals, property sales, and commercial real estate in four languages. Active on Instagram with 1,100+ property posts — one of the most visually documented real estate portfolios in Tokyo.
highlights: Japan's largest English-language real estate agency — 55+ years in Tokyo
Trusted by embassies and Fortune 500 companies for corporate relocations
7,000+ active property listings across Tokyo in 4 languages

title: Ohara & Furukawa
description: Osaka-based firm with deep expertise in international divorce, child custody, and Hague Convention cases. Bilingual staff (English/Japanese). One of the few firms with published Hague Convention expertise. Founded 1979.

title: Setagaya International Law Office
description: Founded in 2020 by Seiya Sato, who trained for six years under international family law veteran Masayuki Honda. Listed on US, Canadian, and UK Embassy referral lists. Specializes in international divorce, custody, visitation, and Hague Convention child return cases. Available 8:00–20:00 including weekends — unusually accessible for a Tokyo law firm.
highlights: Trained under IAFL Fellow Masayuki Honda for 6 years before going independent
Available 7 days a week, 8am to 8pm — rare for Tokyo attorneys
Clients describe him as 'the best English-speaking lawyer in Tokyo's 23 wards'

title: Verybest Law Offices (Divorce)
description: Japan's largest law firm (~400 lawyers, 75 offices nationwide). Handles consensual, mediated, and litigated divorce, child custody, property division, and Hague Convention cases. Free 30-minute initial consultation. English and Chinese support.
address: MFPR Roppongi Azabudai Bldg. 11F, 1-8-7 Roppongi, Minato-ku, Tokyo

title: Masayuki Honda International Law Office
description: Led by Masayuki Honda, a 29-year veteran of international family law who built legal infrastructure in Cambodia with JICA and represented Japan at the UN Vienna Centre before focusing on cross-border family disputes in Tokyo. Won a landmark shared physical custody ruling in Tokyo Family Court based on foreign law, upheld by the Supreme Court in 2021. One of the few attorneys in Japan with deep expertise in both Hague Convention child return cases and international custody litigation.
highlights: Won Supreme Court-upheld shared custody ruling based on foreign law — a first in Japan
JICA legal advisor in Cambodia, UN Vienna Centre representative
Featured in Nikkei, NHK, and Kyodo News coverage of international family law

title: Tokyo J Law Office
description: Founded in 2010 by Eriko Matsuno, who spent a decade at Morgan Stanley and Nagashima Ohno Tsunematsu before specializing in international family law. Served on the founding board of WealthNavi (now publicly traded) and currently serves as its outside director. Handles international divorce, custody disputes, Hague Convention child return cases, and ADR/mediation. Also serves as arbitrator at Tokyo Bar Association for international family disputes.
highlights: Morgan Stanley and Nagashima Ohno background — rare corporate-to-family law pivot
International family dispute arbitrator at Tokyo Bar Association
Deep expertise in Japan's new joint custody law (effective April 2026)

title: Tokyo Sanno Law Office
description: Handles international divorce, child custody & visitation, marital property distribution, and Hague Convention cases. Offers teleconference/video consultations. Located in Roppongi, Ark Hills.

title: ACROSEED Immigration Lawyer's Office
description: Leading immigration firm since 1986 with 99.9% approval rate. Handles all visa types, permanent residence, naturalization, and business setup. English and Chinese support. Free consultations available.

title: Samurai Immigration Law Firm
description: Immigration specialists with 7 offices across Japan (Tokyo, Yokohama, Osaka, Nagoya). Free consultations, weekend appointments available. Handles all visa categories including work, family, and permanent residence.
address: 4F VORT UENO BLD, Ueno 7-4-7, Taito-ku, Tokyo

title: June Advisors Group (Juridique.jp)
description: Bilingual professionals assisting with visa applications, company incorporation, and business setup in Japan. Joint legal office with consulting services for market entry.

title: Oak Admin Office
description: Run by Ayako Smith, a bilingual administrative scrivener who builds her own tools — including a Highly Skilled Professional (HSP) visa point calculator covering all three categories. Specializes in all visa types, permanent residence, Certificate of Eligibility, and Business Manager Visas. Also handles entertainment visas for touring musicians and bands, a niche few other firms touch.
highlights: Built a custom HSP point calculator — the only one of its kind from a scrivener in Japan
Niche expertise in entertainment visas for international touring artists
Publishes immigration policy updates within days of government announcements

title: Atsumi & Sakai (APLaw Japan)
description: Full-service Tokyo law firm with 200+ lawyers including 23 foreign attorneys. Specializes in corporate law, labor/employment, M&A, and cross-border transactions. Most lawyers conduct business in English.
address: Fukoku Seimei Bldg. 16F, 2-2-2 Uchisaiwaicho, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo 100-0011

title: Sumikawa Law Office
description: Led by Kei Sumikawa, who scored a perfect 990 on the TOEIC after growing up for seven years at an American school in the Netherlands. Serves as guest professor at Yokohama National University, lecturing on Japanese law in English for international students. Covers corporate law, contracts, labor disputes, inheritance, divorce, real estate, and bankruptcy — all without a translator. Currently Kawasaki Bar Association chapter president and collaborates with law firms in 10+ countries.
highlights: Native-level English from 7 years at an American school in the Netherlands
Kawasaki Bar Association chapter president — deep local legal network
Partners with law firms in 10+ countries for cross-border matters

title: Lawyer Yasuhiro Fujii
description: Bilingual employment and labor law specialist with 25+ years experience. Advises on dismissal, wage disputes, harassment, contracts, and restructuring for both employers and employees.
address: Toranomon Suzuki Bldg. 4F, 3-20-4 Toranomon, Minato-ku, Tokyo

title: Verybest Law Offices (Labor)
description: Japan's largest law firm handling employment disputes for foreigners. Covers unfair dismissal, employment contracts, labor tribunal representation, and performance disputes. English and Chinese support.
address: MFPR Roppongi Azabudai Bldg. 11F, 1-8-7 Roppongi, Minato-ku, Tokyo

title: Hattori Law Firm
description: Business law firm in Tokyo supporting foreign companies and individuals. Specializes in English-language contract negotiation, corporate legal matters, and international business transactions in Japan.
address: Roppongi Hills North Tower 3F, 6-2-31 Roppongi, Minato-ku, Tokyo

title: Tokyo Medical and Surgical Clinic
description: Founded in 1951 by British physician Dr. Wedderburn and American Dr. Gentry — six years before Tokyo Tower was built. For 75 years, TMSC has remained independently owned by its physician partners, serving Tokyo's international community with European and US-trained doctors. Full general practice including internal medicine, dermatology, pediatrics, psychotherapy, and minor surgery. One of their endoscopy specialists has performed over 20,000 procedures.
highlights: Tokyo's longest-running international clinic — serving expats since 1951
Independently owned by physician partners, never acquired by a hospital group
Endoscopy specialist with 20,000+ procedures; acupuncturist formerly with Liverpool FC

title: American Clinic Tokyo
description: Originally opened next to the US Embassy in 1954 by Dr. Shane, who ran it for 60 years. Now led by Dr. Grant Mikasa, a Japanese-American physician who attended St. Mary's International School and Teikyo University School of Medicine. 64% of patients are from the United States, with 20+ nationalities represented. Offers general medicine, orthopedics, vaccinations, telemedicine, and hotel house calls — recently expanded with a hotel doctor service in Sapporo.
highlights: Founded in 1954 — one of Tokyo's oldest clinics serving the international community
Dr. Mikasa is a TEDx speaker and published medical researcher (3 papers, 33 citations)
74% of consultations done via telemedicine — maximum convenience for expats

title: Tokyo Station International Clinic
description: Founded in 2016 by Dr. Sho Naito, who spent three years as the sole physician on Iriomote Island before earning an MBA at Keio University. Grew from a single clinic to an 11-location, 250-person network in just eight years — opening three new locations in 2024 alone. Developed a proprietary electronic health record system called 'Clinicalte.' Open daily 9AM–9PM, serving 700+ international patients monthly with English and Chinese-speaking doctors.
highlights: 11-clinic network built in 8 years — one of Japan's fastest-growing medical groups
Proprietary EHR system 'Clinicalte' developed in-house
Open 365 days a year, 9AM–9PM — no appointment needed for walk-ins

title: Raffles Medical Osaka
description: Part of Singapore's largest private healthcare group. Family medicine, travel medicine, dermatology, health screenings. English, Chinese, and Japanese-speaking doctors and staff. Located in Herbis Osaka, Umeda.

title: Argentum Wealth Management
description: Japan's largest licensed independent financial advisory firm for expats. Financial planning, investments, retirement, insurance, and estate planning. Licensed by Japanese Ministry of Finance. English, Japanese, Chinese, and Spanish support.
address: Nisso 22 Bldg, Unit 802, 1-11-10 Azabudai, Minato-ku, Tokyo

title: Otani Accounting Office
description: Founded in 1983 by David Otani with the philosophy 'For foreigners. By foreigners.' In 2017, became the first firm in Japan to offer English-language tax services at market-rate prices — ending the era of premium pricing for foreign residents. Now serves 3,600+ clients across 25 countries with a team of 6, offering company incorporation, tax filing, payroll, and bookkeeping. One-stop service with bilingual lawyers, registered agents, and European tax advisors.
highlights: First firm to offer English tax services at Japanese market rates — not expat premium
3,600+ clients across 25 countries — one of Japan's largest foreigner-focused practices
One-stop service: bilingual lawyers, registered agents, and European tax advisors on staff

title: Kaori Fuchi Tax and Consulting
description: Boutique international tax firm founded by ex-Ernst & Young certified tax accountant. English accounting, tax filing, bookkeeping, and payroll for foreign companies and individuals in Japan.
address: Portal Point Shibuya 104, 4-17 Sakuragaoka, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0031

title: YASUDA Accounting
description: English-speaking accounting firm specializing in international taxation. Services for foreign companies and individuals in Japan including tax returns, bookkeeping, and business consulting.

title: Kyno Tax Office
description: Founded by Keisho Kayano, who worked at EY's Dusseldorf office handling Brexit-related international tax cases before returning to Japan to start his own practice in 2020. Now processes 100+ stock option and RSU/ESPP tax filings per year — one of the highest volumes in Japan for this niche. Specializes in the exact tax situations facing tech workers at foreign companies: stock options, RSUs, ESPPs, and international income. Consultations available via Zoom, Google Meet, and LINE.
highlights: Former EY Dusseldorf — handled Brexit international tax cases at Big 4 level
100+ stock option/RSU filings per year — deep niche in tech worker taxation
Fully remote consultations via Zoom, Google Meet, and LINE

title: Ken Corporation
description: Leading Tokyo real estate agency with 50+ years experience and 7,000+ properties. Specializes in premium expat housing and corporate relocation. 30 fluent English-speaking agents. Trusted by embassies and global companies.

title: AtHearth
description: Foreigner-friendly apartment platform with 2,000+ curated Tokyo listings. No guarantor needed. AI-powered matching, English support, and digital applications. Served 5,000+ expats since 2015.
address: Imperial Ochanomizu #225, 3-11-2 Kanda Ogawamachi, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo 101-0052

title: GaijinPot Apartments
description: Japan's largest English-language apartment search platform for foreigners. Nationwide coverage with focus on Tokyo and Osaka. No guarantor or Japanese bank account needed. Apply from overseas.

title: Sun Realty & Insurance
description: Pioneering luxury expat housing agency with nearly 70 years in Tokyo. First agency to offer premium housing to foreign residents. Japanese and English-speaking agents for rentals, sales, and property management.

title: Urushimatsu Tax Accountant Office
description: Founded by Yuko Urushimatsu, a former EY (Ernst & Young) Expats tax team member who left Big 4 to focus exclusively on individual international tax for foreign residents. Contributed two widely-cited columns to The Japan Times on OAR reporting obligations and inheritance tax reform. Recently stopped accepting corporate clients to focus entirely on individual international tax — a rare level of specialization in this field.
highlights: Former EY (Ernst & Young) Expats tax team — Big 4 international tax expertise
Japan Times contributor on OAR reporting and inheritance tax reform
100% focused on individual international tax — no corporate clients

title: Toshiko Takahashi Tax Office
description: Led by Toshiko Takahashi, who left a career at Sumitomo Metal to pass the full certified tax accountant exam at age 36. Ran a solo practice for 15 years before expanding to a five-person team in 2024. About 20% of her clients are foreign nationals, primarily in Niseko, Furano, and Fukagawa — making her one of the few English-speaking tax professionals serving Hokkaido's growing international community. Member of the Hokkaido Tax CPA Association International Committee.
highlights: One of very few English-speaking tax professionals in Hokkaido
20% foreign clients — deep experience with Niseko/Furano property and business tax
Expanded from solo practice to 5-person team in 2024
